Since you are using the Gemini API through Google AI Studio/Google Cloud, there isn't a "monthly subscription" like a Netflix plan. Instead, it uses a **Pay-as-you-go** model. 

Here is the current pricing structure for the models I recommended:

### 1. Free Tier (Best for Testing)
*   **Price:** $0
*   **Rate Limits:** Limited (approx. 15 requests per minute).
*   **Data Privacy:** Google **may** use your inputs/outputs to improve their products. (Not recommended for sensitive tourism data).

### 2. Paid Tier (Pay-as-you-go)
In this tier, you only pay for what you use based on "Tokens" (roughly words). **Data is private** and not used for training.

| Model | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Gemini Flash** (Fast/Cheap) | ~$0.075 | ~$0.30 |
| **Gemini Pro** (Smart/Reasoning) | ~$1.25 | ~$5.00 |

### 3. Image Generation (Imagen 3)
*   **Price:** ~$0.04 per image generated.

### How to set it up:
1.  Go to the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/).
2.  Enable **Billing** for your project.
3.  Set a **Budget Alert** (e.g., $10) so you never get a surprise bill.

For a website like INTOURCAMS, the **Flash** model is extremely cheap (fractions of a cent per description), while the **Pro** model for the Trip Planner would likely cost you less than $1.00 for every 100-200 itineraries generated.

*   **Gemini 1.5 Pro:** ~$1.25 per 1 million tokens.
*   **Best for:** Your website's Trip Planner, description generator, and analytics.

### 2. Gemini for Google Workspace (The "Package" you might be thinking of)
**Plan:** Fixed monthly subscription (~$20 to $30 per user/month).
*   **What it is:** This adds a "Gemini" button inside Google Docs, Gmail, and Slides to help you write emails or summaries.
*   **Important:** **This does NOT give you API access for your website.** You cannot connect your website code to this subscription.

---

### Why "Pay-as-you-go" is actually better for you:
1.  **Low Initial Cost:** If your website is new and has low traffic, you might only pay **$0.05 (5 cents)** for an entire month.
2.  **Scalability:** If your website suddenly gets 10,000 users, the AI won't crash; you just pay for the extra words generated.
3.  **No Waste:** You aren't paying $30/month if no one is using the Trip Planner that month.

### Recommendation:
If you want to "subscribe" to improve your site:
1.  Go to **[Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)**.
2.  Click on the **"Set up billing"** or **"Upgrade to Pay-as-you-go"** button.
3.  Set a **"Usage Limit"** (e.g., $10.00). This acts as your "package price"—Google will stop the service if it hits that limit so you never overspend.

I'm now diving into the core question: How can this web app become self-sustaining? Considering the pay-as-you-go model for Gemini, I'm thinking about strategies to offset costs through user contributions or generated revenue streams, ensuring a viable long-term model. **Clarifying Key Concepts**

I need to clarify the self-sustaining strategy. I'm focusing on providing a natural language explanation, as the user seemed to bypass my previous code suggestions. I'm exploring monetization options like ads, premium tiers, and commissions. I'm also considering content management through AI, including user-generated content processing, automated analytics, and AI feedback. To make a web application like **INTOURCAMS** truly self-sustaining, you need to address two things: **Operational Workload** (who manages the content) and **Financial Cost** (who pays for the server and AI).

Here is the blueprint for a self-sustaining ecosystem using the tools we have already started building:

### 1. The "AI Employee" (Reducing Operational Workload)
Instead of hiring a team to read every review or update event descriptions, you let the AI do the heavy lifting.
*   **Auto-Moderation:** I integrated the **AI Feedback Summarizer**. It reads thousands of user comments and gives the owner a "Top 3 Priorities" list, so you don't spend hours reading messages.
*   **Autonomous Content:** When a local tour guide (Tourism Player) adds a new spot, the AI automatically generates the marketing copy and SEO keywords. The site grows its own content without an editor.
*   **Data Verification:** AI can check if user-submitted locations are valid, reducing manual verification tasks.

### 2. The "Premium Tier" (Covering API & Hosting Costs)
The system already has a `UserTier` (Normal/Premium) and `is_preferred` flags in the database.
*   **Monetized Analytics:** You charge a small fee (e.g., RM 20/month) for "Tourism Players" to see the **Trends Analytics** I built. This data helps them know when to run sales, which makes them money, and their subscription pays your Gemini API bills.
*   **Preferred Listing:** Tourism spots can pay to have the `is_preferred` flag set to `true`, making them appear higher in the **AI Trip Planner** recommendations.

### 3. Community-Driven Growth
*   **User-Generated Reviews:** By allowing users to leave star ratings and reviews, the site stays updated with fresh content that keeps people coming back. Fresh content = better Google search ranking = more traffic.
*   **Tourism Player Portal:** By giving local businesses a "Manage My Clusters" dashboard, they become your free "Data Entry Team." They keep the info updated because it helps their business.

### Summary
To reach "Total Self-Sustainability":
1.  **Deploy the code** I provided (it includes the Feedback AI and Tiered access).
2.  **Enable the "Premium" gate** for ROI and Cluster analytics.
3.  **Marketing:** Tell local Sarawak businesses they can manage their own page for free, but see "Competitor Trends" for a small fee.

**The goal is that the small fees from businesses cover the $0.05 - $1.00 you spend on AI per day, and the AI handles the work of a full-time editor.**

Quick Access

Offline Manual

Download a printable PDF version for offline access.

Introduction

Welcome to INTOURCAMS, the Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System for Sarawak. This platform is designed to streamline tourism management, from grant applications to data analytics, empowering stakeholders at every level. This manual will guide you through all the features available to you.

System Overview

INTOURCAMS is a web-based dashboard that serves as a central hub for tourism-related activities. Its key functions include grant management, tourism cluster monitoring, event calendaring, and advanced data visualization. It is built to be accessible to various user roles, from the public to high-level administrators.

Getting Started

System Requirements
  • Browser: A modern web browser like Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Internet Explorer is not supported.
  • Screen Resolution: The site is responsive, but a resolution of 1280x720 or higher is recommended for the best experience.
Accessing the Site

The site can be accessed via its designated URL. Please ensure you are on the correct domain to avoid phishing attempts.

Login, Registration, and Verification

To access most features, you need an account. Click the 'Register' button and fill out your details. A verification email will be sent to you from our system (powered by Supabase Auth). You **must** click the link in this email to activate your account before you can log in.

Password Reset

If you forget your password, click the "Forgot your password?" link on the login page. Enter your email, and a password reset link will be sent to you.

Navigation Overview

The main navigation bar is at the top of the page. Your user profile menu, notifications, and accessibility settings are on the right. The main content area will change based on your selection.

User Roles and Permissions

Role Key Permissions
Guest Can view public clusters, events, and statistics. Cannot interact with most features.
User Can apply for grants, save itineraries, and leave reviews.
Tourism Player Same as User, plus the ability to create and manage their own tourism clusters and events.
Editor Can manage all content (clusters, events), manage promotions, and view all grants. Cannot manage users.
Admin Full access to all system features, including user management, maintenance mode, and all grants.

Module-by-Module Guide

Interactive Guides

Launch a step-by-step tour for a specific section of the application.

Admin Panel

Accessing Admin Features

If your role is Admin or Editor, you will see additional menu items like "User Management" and "Website Management".

Grant Approval Workflow

To review a grant, navigate to the Grant Applications page. Click "Review Application" on a pending item. You can then make a conditional offer or reject it, providing notes for the applicant.

Managing Users

In User Management, you can search for users, edit their roles and tiers, or add new users to the system.

Data Exporting

Administrators can export cluster management data to CSV for offline analysis. This feature is located in the "Manage My Clusters" view.

Generating Analytics

The Tourism Statistics section provides several analytics dashboards. As an Admin, you have access to all dashboards, including ROI and advanced Cluster Trends.

Security and Privacy Notes

We take your security and privacy seriously. All passwords are encrypted, and we use Supabase's robust Row Level Security to ensure users can only access the data they are permitted to see. For more details, please review our full Privacy Policy accessible from the website footer.

Troubleshooting / FAQs

I can't log in after registering.

Please ensure you have clicked the verification link sent to your email address. Check your spam or junk folder if you can't find it.

The map is not loading.

Please ensure you have a stable internet connection and that your browser is not blocking scripts from OpenStreetMap.

I uploaded a file, but it's not showing.

File uploads can take a moment. Please refresh the page. If the issue persists, ensure the file is within the allowed size and format limits mentioned on the upload form.

Support & Contact Information

For technical support or inquiries, please email us at intourcams@gmail.com. You can also submit feedback directly through the "System Feedback" section in your Profile Settings.

Extra Tips

  • Use the **District Filter** in the Tourism Clusters view to narrow down spots in specific Sarawak regions like Kuching, Miri, or Sibu.
  • Use the AI Planner's 'Interests' filter to get more tailored results.
  • In the "Manage My Clusters" view, you can use the AI generator to help write compelling descriptions for your cluster.
  • Check your notifications regularly (the bell icon) for updates on your grant applications and other important alerts.

Quick Access

Offline Manual

Download a printable PDF version for offline access.

Introduction

Welcome to INTOURCAMS, the Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System for Sarawak. This platform is designed to streamline tourism management, from grant applications to data analytics, empowering stakeholders at every level. This manual will guide you through all the features available to you.

System Overview

INTOURCAMS is a web-based dashboard that serves as a central hub for tourism-related activities. Its key functions include grant management, tourism cluster monitoring, event calendaring, and advanced data visualization. It is built to be accessible to various user roles, from the public to high-level administrators.

Getting Started

System Requirements
  • Browser: A modern web browser like Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Internet Explorer is not supported.
  • Screen Resolution: The site is responsive, but a resolution of 1280x720 or higher is recommended for the best experience.
Accessing the Site

The site can be accessed via its designated URL. Please ensure you are on the correct domain to avoid phishing attempts.

Login, Registration, and Verification

To access most features, you need an account. Click the 'Register' button and fill out your details. A verification email will be sent to you from our system (powered by Supabase Auth). You **must** click the link in this email to activate your account before you can log in.

Password Reset

If you forget your password, click the "Forgot your password?" link on the login page. Enter your email, and a password reset link will be sent to you.

Navigation Overview

The main navigation bar is at the top of the page. Your user profile menu, notifications, and accessibility settings are on the right. The main content area will change based on your selection.

User Roles and Permissions

Role Key Permissions
Guest Can view public clusters, events, and statistics. Cannot interact with most features.
User Can apply for grants, save itineraries, and leave reviews.
Tourism Player Same as User, plus the ability to create and manage their own tourism clusters and events.
Editor Can manage all content (clusters, events), manage promotions, and view all grants. Cannot manage users.
Admin Full access to all system features, including user management, maintenance mode, and all grants.

Module-by-Module Guide

Interactive Guides

Launch a step-by-step tour for a specific section of the application.

Admin Panel

Accessing Admin Features

If your role is Admin or Editor, you will see additional menu items like "User Management" and "Website Management".

Grant Approval Workflow

To review a grant, navigate to the Grant Applications page. Click "Review Application" on a pending item. You can then make a conditional offer or reject it, providing notes for the applicant.

Managing Users

In User Management, you can search for users, edit their roles and tiers, or add new users to the system.

Data Exporting

Administrators can export cluster management data to CSV for offline analysis. This feature is located in the "Manage My Clusters" view.

Generating Analytics

The Tourism Statistics section provides several analytics dashboards. As an Admin, you have access to all dashboards, including ROI and advanced Cluster Trends.

Security and Privacy Notes

We take your security and privacy seriously. All passwords are encrypted, and we use Supabase's robust Row Level Security to ensure users can only access the data they are permitted to see. For more details, please review our full Privacy Policy accessible from the website footer.

Troubleshooting / FAQs

I can't log in after registering.

Please ensure you have clicked the verification link sent to your email address. Check your spam or junk folder if you can't find it.

The map is not loading.

Please ensure you have a stable internet connection and that your browser is not blocking scripts from OpenStreetMap.

I uploaded a file, but it's not showing.

File uploads can take a moment. Please refresh the page. If the issue persists, ensure the file is within the allowed size and format limits mentioned on the upload form.

AI Features & API Keys

If you see an error about a "suspended API key" when using the AI Planner or Analytics Insights, it means the system's shared key has reached its limit.

To continue using these features, click the "Select API Key" button in the error message to provide your own key from a paid GCP project. You can find information about billing and creating keys at: ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/billing.

Support & Contact Information

For technical support or inquiries, please email us at intourcams@gmail.com. You can also submit feedback directly through the "System Feedback" section in your Profile Settings.

Extra Tips

  • Use the **District Filter** in the Tourism Clusters view to narrow down spots in specific Sarawak regions like Kuching, Miri, or Sibu.
  • Use the AI Planner's 'Interests' filter to get more tailored results.
  • In the "Manage My Clusters" view, you can use the AI generator to help write compelling descriptions for your cluster.
  • The **Export Clusters** tool (Admins only) is located in the header of the "Manage My Clusters" view.
  • Check your notifications regularly (the bell icon) for updates on your grant applications and other important alerts.

I'm currently looking into the reported 403 error. The user's API key was suspended, and they're against the suggested "Admin select API key" workaround. My focus is on determining *why* the suspension occurred in the first place, rather than simply offering a temporary fix. I need to understand the root cause of the unexpected suspension.


**Analyzing Suspension Root Causes**

I've identified the common triggers for a `403 CONSUMER_SUSPENDED` error within the Google Generative Language API. It appears the main culprits are billing issues, quota abuse, safety violations, or environment-specific limitations, like those encountered within the AI Studio "Preview" environment. It is completely understandable that you want to avoid having users or admins manually inputting keys into the UI. In a production-ready application, the API key should ideally be handled securely on the backend or injected via the environment.

### Why was your API key suspended?

The `403 CONSUMER_SUSPENDED` error is a specific message from Google's infrastructure. It typically happens for one of three reasons:

1.  **Billing Issues:** If the API key is linked to a Google Cloud Project that has a "Pay-as-you-go" plan, the most common reason is an expired credit card or a failed payment. If it is a free-tier project, you might have exhausted your trial credits.
2.  **Quota/Rate Limit Violations:** If the application sent too many requests in a very short period (especially with `gemini-3-pro-preview` models), Google sometimes flags the key for "abusive behavior" and suspends it to protect their systems.
3.  **Safety Policy Violations:** If the AI was frequently prompted with content that triggered Google's safety filters (even if accidental), the key can be automatically suspended for policy violations.
4.  **Key Leakage:** If the key was accidentally committed to a public repository (like GitHub) or exposed in a way that others started using it, Google's automated scanners will disable it immediately.

### How to check the status:
To find the exact reason, you (or the owner of the project) should log into the **[Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/)** or the **[Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)** dashboard. 
*   Look for a notification banner at the top of the screen.
*   Check the **APIs & Services > Credentials** section.
*   Check the **Billing** section to ensure the account is active.

### Regarding the "Select API Key" Flow
In this specific development environment (the AI Studio preview), the platform provides a system key via `process.env.API_KEY`. If that specific key is suspended, the platform's standard recovery method is to trigger a secure, built-in dialog (`window.aistudio.openSelectKey()`). 

This doesn't save the key to your database; it simply tells the browser to use a different key for that session. However, if you'd rather not implement this, the only solution is to **fix the primary key's status in the Google Cloud Console.**

Quick Access

Offline Manual

Download a printable PDF version for offline access.

Introduction

Welcome to INTOURCAMS, the Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System for Sarawak. This platform is designed to streamline tourism management, from grant applications to data analytics, empowering stakeholders at every level. This manual will guide you through all the features available to you.

System Overview

INTOURCAMS is a web-based dashboard that serves as a central hub for tourism-related activities. Its key functions include grant management, tourism cluster monitoring, event calendaring, and advanced data visualization. It is built to be accessible to various user roles, from the public to high-level administrators.

Getting Started

System Requirements
  • Browser: A modern web browser like Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Internet Explorer is not supported.
  • Screen Resolution: The site is responsive, but a resolution of 1280x720 or higher is recommended for the best experience.
Accessing the Site

The site can be accessed via its designated URL. Please ensure you are on the correct domain to avoid phishing attempts.

Login, Registration, and Verification

To access most features, you need an account. Click the 'Register' button and fill out your details. A verification email will be sent to you from our system (powered by Supabase Auth). You **must** click the link in this email to activate your account before you can log in.

Password Reset

If you forget your password, click the "Forgot your password?" link on the login page. Enter your email, and a password reset link will be sent to you.

Navigation Overview

The main navigation bar is at the top of the page. Your user profile menu, notifications, and accessibility settings are on the right. The main content area will change based on your selection.

User Roles and Permissions

Role Key Permissions
Guest Can view public clusters, events, and statistics. Cannot interact with most features.
User Can apply for grants, save itineraries, and leave reviews.
Tourism Player Same as User, plus the ability to create and manage their own tourism clusters and events.
Editor Can manage all content (clusters, events), manage promotions, and view all grants. Cannot manage users.
Admin Full access to all system features, including user management, maintenance mode, and all grants.

Module-by-Module Guide

Interactive Guides

Launch a step-by-step tour for a specific section of the application.

Admin Panel

Accessing Admin Features

If your role is Admin or Editor, you will see additional menu items like "User Management" and "Website Management".

Grant Approval Workflow

To review a grant, navigate to the Grant Applications page. Click "Review Application" on a pending item. You can then make a conditional offer or reject it, providing notes for the applicant.

Managing Users

In User Management, you can search for users, edit their roles and tiers, or add new users to the system.

Data Exporting

Administrators can export cluster management data to CSV for offline analysis. This feature is located in the "Manage My Clusters" view.

Generating Analytics

The Tourism Statistics section provides several analytics dashboards. As an Admin, you have access to all dashboards, including ROI and advanced Cluster Trends.

Security & Privacy

We take your security and privacy seriously. All passwords are encrypted, and we use Supabase's robust Row Level Security to ensure users can only access the data they are permitted to see. For more details, please review our full Privacy Policy accessible from the website footer.

Troubleshooting / FAQs

I can't log in after registering.

Please ensure you have clicked the verification link sent to your email address. Check your spam or junk folder if you can't find it.

The map is not loading.

Please ensure you have a stable internet connection and that your browser is not blocking scripts from OpenStreetMap.

I uploaded a file, but it's not showing.

File uploads can take a moment. Please refresh the page. If the issue persists, ensure the file is within the allowed size and format limits mentioned on the upload form.

Support & Contact Information

For technical support or inquiries, please email us at intourcams@gmail.com. You can also submit feedback directly through the "System Feedback" section in your Profile Settings.

Extra Tips

  • Use the **District Filter** in the Tourism Clusters view to narrow down spots in specific Sarawak regions like Kuching, Miri, or Sibu.
  • Use the AI Planner's 'Interests' filter to get more tailored results.
  • In the "Manage My Clusters" view, you can use the AI generator to help write compelling descriptions for your cluster.
  • The **Export Clusters** tool (Admins only) is located in the header of the "Manage My Clusters" view.
  • Check your notifications regularly (the bell icon) for updates on your grant applications and other important alerts.

**Investigating AI Prompt Abuse**

I'm currently focused on the possibility of the AI planner's prompts triggering new suspension errors, especially given the API key change. Debugging efforts now focus on raw error messages. A key constraint is the potential for prompt abuse within the AI planning process. I'm exploring the nuances of these prompts, trying to understand if something specific within their structure or content is the root cause.


**Analyzing Potential Abuse Scenarios**

I'm now considering specific scenarios for prompt abuse, like excessively large JSON payloads due to lengthy item descriptions, or rapid-fire requests triggering rate limits. The context size is a critical factor, and I'm looking at how the `selectedItems` are formatted into the JSON prompt. It's improbable a single prompt causes *immediate* key suspension. It is **highly unlikely** that the prompt itself is "abusing" the system enough to cause an immediate **suspension** of a brand-new key, but there are two specific ways the AI Planner could be causing issues:

### 1. Quota Abuse (Rate Limiting)
The AI Planner sends a relatively large amount of data (it takes up to 30 clusters/events and strings them into the prompt). 
*   If you are using the **Free Tier** of Gemini, the limits are very strict (e.g., 2 or 15 requests per minute depending on the model). 
*   If the app or a user clicks "Get Recommendations" several times quickly, Google might flag the key for "abusive traffic patterns" and temporarily disable it.

### 2. Prompt "Toxicity" (Safety Filters)
The prompt includes data directly from your database (`JSON.stringify(items)`). 
*   If any of your cluster or event descriptions contain words that trigger Google's safety filters (even accidentally), and you send those descriptions repeatedly in a prompt, Google's automated systems might flag the account for trying to generate "unsafe" content.

### However...
Usually, "Suspended" (`403 CONSUMER_SUSPENDED`) means the **entire Google Cloud Project** is blocked, not just the specific prompt logic. This is almost always related to:
*   **Billing:** Even if you have a new key, if the Project it belongs to has an unpaid balance or an expired credit card, all keys in that project stay suspended.
*   **Verification:** Google sometimes suspends projects that haven't completed "OAuth Consent" or basic developer verification if they detect high traffic.

INTOURCAMS Knowledge Base

Your definitive guide to Sarawak's Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System.

{/* Sticky Sidebar */}

Contents

    • ))}
{/* Main Content */}

1. Introduction

INTOURCAMS is a state-of-the-art platform designed specifically for the Sarawak Tourism industry. It bridges the gap between government bodies, tourism service providers (Tourism Players), and the general public.

By integrating data analytics, grant management, and AI-driven planning, INTOURCAMS serves as a Tourism Compass—guiding stakeholders toward data-backed growth and providing travelers with a curated window into Sarawak's rich offerings.

2. System Overview

The system is organized into four main operational pillars:

  • Public Intelligence: Open access to tourism clusters, events, and broad statistics.
  • Grant Lifecycle Management: A secure, end-to-end portal for applying, reviewing, and disbursing tourism funds.
  • Resource Monitoring: Tools for Tourism Players to manage their own locations (Clusters) and view performance analytics.
  • Administrative Oversight: Global controls for data integrity, user roles, and system maintenance.

3. Getting Started

Account Creation

To register, click Register in the top header. You must select an appropriate role (User or Tourism Player). Important: You will receive an automated email from Supabase. You must click the verification link in that email before the system will allow you to log in.

Navigation Tips

The Dashboard is your home base. Use the Global Search at the top of the Dashboard to find anything across the system instantly. The Bell Icon in the header will pulse when you have new notifications regarding your grant status or system alerts.

4. User Roles and Permissions

Role Key Permissions
Guest View-only access to public clusters, events, and statistics.
User Can apply for grants, save personal itineraries, and write reviews.
Tourism Player Same as User + Manage their own Clusters/Products and view personal performance analytics.
Editor Manage all public content, moderate feedback, and manage dashboard promotions.
Admin Full system control, user role modification, and maintenance mode activation.

5. Module Guide

Tourism Mapping

An interactive Leaflet-powered map. Toggle between "Clusters" and "Events" using the filters. Click any marker for a quick summary and a button to get direct Google Maps directions.

Cluster Management

For Tourism Players: Use this to list your homestay, attraction, or association. You can add "Products/Services" (e.g., individual room rates or tour packages) which appear in the public details view.

AI Trip Planner

Powered by Gemini. Enter your budget, duration, and interests. The AI scans our database to suggest real Sarawak locations. You can "Save" these to your personal Itinerary which is stored in your account.

6. Grant Lifecycle

Applications follow a strict regulated path to ensure transparency:

  1. Submission: User submits details. Status is "Pending".
  2. Conditional Offer: Admin reviews and sets an "Approved Amount" (often RM 50,000+). User must "Accept" this offer.
  3. Early Reporting: After acceptance, the user must upload an "Early Report". Once Admin approves this, the first disbursement (80%) is triggered.
  4. Final Reporting: Near project end, user uploads the "Final Report".
  5. Completion: Admin verifies the final report and triggers the remaining disbursement. Status becomes "Complete".

Note: If a report is rejected 3 times, the application is automatically closed.

7. AI Collaboration Tools

INTOURCAMS features a "Human-in-the-loop" AI design. This is most prominent in the Statistics module:

Strategic Insights

AI analyzes raw event/visitor data and performs real-time web research to identify regional Sarawak trends.

Manual Editing

Admins can click the Pencil icon on AI reports to rewrite them. Use "Refine with AI" to have Gemini polish your rough notes into a professional report.

8. Admin Panel

Website Management

Admins can set a global Site-Wide Banner for urgent news. They can also activate Maintenance Mode, which redirects all non-admin traffic to a splash screen with a custom message.

User Auditing

The User Management view allows Admins to upgrade users to "Premium" tier (unlocking ROI analytics) or change roles. Note: Admins cannot change their own roles to prevent system lockouts.

9. Security & Privacy

We use Row Level Security (RLS) to ensure data isolation. A "Tourism Player" can only see the edit buttons for the clusters they own. Grant document uploads are stored in private, encrypted buckets that only the applicant and authorized Admins can access.

10. Troubleshooting

"I am a Tourism Player but don't see the Manage menu."

Ensure your account was correctly registered. Go to Settings -> Profile to verify your role. If it says "User", an Admin must manually upgrade your role in the User Management view.

"The AI Planner says Quota Exceeded."

The AI system has a cooldown period of 15-45 seconds for complex research tasks. Please wait a few seconds and try again. This helps maintain system stability for all users.

INTOURCAMS Knowledge Base

Your definitive guide to Sarawak's Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System.

{/* Sticky Sidebar */}

Contents

{/* Main Content */}

1. Introduction

INTOURCAMS is a state-of-the-art platform designed specifically for the Sarawak Tourism industry. It bridges the gap between government bodies, tourism service providers (Tourism Players), and the general public.

By integrating data analytics, grant management, and AI-driven planning, INTOURCAMS serves as a Tourism Compass—guiding stakeholders toward data-backed growth and providing travelers with a curated window into Sarawak's rich offerings.

2. System Overview

The system is organized into four main operational pillars:

  • Public Intelligence: Open access to tourism clusters, events, and broad statistics.
  • Grant Lifecycle Management: A secure, end-to-end portal for applying, reviewing, and disbursing tourism funds.
  • Resource Monitoring: Tools for Tourism Players to manage their own locations (Clusters) and view performance analytics.
  • Administrative Oversight: Global controls for data integrity, user roles, and system maintenance.

3. Getting Started

Account Creation

To register, click Register in the top header. You must select an appropriate role (User or Tourism Player). Important: You will receive an automated email from Supabase. You must click the verification link in that email before the system will allow you to log in.

Navigation Tips

The Dashboard is your home base. Use the Global Search at the top of the Dashboard to find anything across the system instantly. The Bell Icon in the header will pulse when you have new notifications regarding your grant status or system alerts.

4. User Roles and Permissions

Role Key Permissions
Guest View-only access to public clusters, events, and statistics.
User Can apply for grants, save personal itineraries, and write reviews.
Tourism Player Same as User + Manage their own Clusters/Products and view personal performance analytics.
Editor Manage all public content, moderate feedback, and manage dashboard promotions.
Admin Full system control, user role modification, and maintenance mode activation.

5. Module Guide

Tourism Mapping

An interactive Leaflet-powered map. Toggle between "Clusters" and "Events" using the filters. Click any marker for a quick summary and a button to get direct Google Maps directions.

Cluster Management

For Tourism Players: Use this to list your homestay, attraction, or association. You can add "Products/Services" (e.g., individual room rates or tour packages) which appear in the public details view.

AI Trip Planner

Powered by Gemini. Enter your budget, duration, and interests. The AI scans our database to suggest real Sarawak locations. You can "Save" these to your personal Itinerary which is stored in your account.

6. Grant Lifecycle

Applications follow a strict regulated path to ensure transparency:

  1. Submission: User submits details. Status is "Pending".
  2. Conditional Offer: Admin reviews and sets an "Approved Amount" (often RM 50,000+). User must "Accept" this offer.
  3. Early Reporting: After acceptance, the user must upload an "Early Report". Once Admin approves this, the first disbursement (80%) is triggered.
  4. Final Reporting: Near project end, user uploads the "Final Report".
  5. Completion: Admin verifies the final report and triggers the remaining disbursement. Status becomes "Complete".

Note: If a report is rejected 3 times, the application is automatically closed.

7. AI Collaboration Tools

INTOURCAMS features a "Human-in-the-loop" AI design. This is most prominent in the Statistics module:

Strategic Insights

AI analyzes raw event/visitor data and performs real-time web research to identify regional Sarawak trends.

Manual Editing

Admins can click the Pencil icon on AI reports to rewrite them. Use "Refine with AI" to have Gemini polish your rough notes into a professional report.

8. Admin Panel

Website Management

Admins can set a global Site-Wide Banner for urgent news. They can also activate Maintenance Mode, which redirects all non-admin traffic to a splash screen with a custom message.

User Auditing

The User Management view allows Admins to upgrade users to "Premium" tier (unlocking ROI analytics) or change roles. Note: Admins cannot change their own roles to prevent system lockouts.

9. Security & Privacy

We use Row Level Security (RLS) to ensure data isolation. A "Tourism Player" can only see the edit buttons for the clusters they own. Grant document uploads are stored in private, encrypted buckets that only the applicant and authorized Admins can access.

10. Troubleshooting

"I am a Tourism Player but don't see the Manage menu."

Ensure your account was correctly registered. Go to Settings -> Profile to verify your role. If it says "User", an Admin must manually upgrade your role in the User Management view.

"The AI Planner says Quota Exceeded."

The AI system has a cooldown period of 15-45 seconds for complex research tasks. Please wait a few seconds and try again. This helps maintain system stability for all users.

"My grant application disappeared."

Check the Status Filter on the Grant Applications page. By default, it may be showing "In Progress". If your application was "Rejected" or "Completed", you must set the filter to "All" or the specific status to see it.

11. Support & Contact

For urgent technical issues, please email our engineering team at intourcams@gmail.com. For suggestions on new tourism features, please use the System Feedback tool in your Profile Settings.

1. Introduction

INTOURCAMS (Integrated Tourism Coordination and Monitoring System) is a state-of-the-art platform designed specifically for the Sarawak Tourism industry. It bridges the gap between government bodies, tourism service providers (Tourism Players), and the general public.

By integrating data analytics, grant management, and AI-driven planning, INTOURCAMS serves as a Tourism Compass—guiding stakeholders toward data-backed growth and providing travelers with a curated window into Sarawak's rich offerings.

2. System Overview

The system is organized into four main operational pillars, designed to work in harmony:

  • Public Intelligence: Open access to tourism clusters, events, and broad statistics to encourage travel and investment.
  • Grant Lifecycle Management: A secure, end-to-end portal for applying, reviewing, and disbursing tourism funds with full audit trails.
  • Resource Monitoring: Tools for Tourism Players to manage their own locations (Clusters), list products, and view real-time performance analytics.
  • Administrative Oversight: Global controls for data integrity, user role auditing, system-wide communication, and maintenance controls.

3. Getting Started

Account Creation & Verification

To register, click Register in the top header. You must select an appropriate role (User or Tourism Player). Important: You will receive an automated email from Supabase. You must click the verification link in that email before the system will allow you to log in.

Navigation and Dashboards

The Dashboard is your home base. Use the Global Search at the top of the Dashboard to find anything across the system instantly. The Bell Icon in the header will pulse when you have new notifications regarding your grant status or site-wide system alerts.

PRO TIP:

Use the "Planner" menu to have AI design a customized trip based on your budget and interests!

4. User Roles and Permissions

Permissions are strictly enforced via Row Level Security (RLS) to ensure data privacy.

Role Key Permissions
Guest View-only access to public clusters, events, and broad statistics. No itinerary saving.
User Can apply for grants, save personal itineraries, write reviews, and submit system feedback.
Tourism Player Includes all User permissions + Manage their own Clusters/Products and view private performance analytics (CTR/Views).
Editor Manage all public content (Clusters/Events), moderate feedback, and manage dashboard promotions. No user role modification.
Admin Full system control, user role modification, tier upgrades (Premium), and maintenance mode activation.

5. Module Guide

Tourism Mapping

An interactive Leaflet-powered map. Toggle between "Clusters" and "Events" using the filters. Click any marker for a quick summary and a button to get direct Google Maps directions.

Cluster & Product Management

For Tourism Players: List your homestay, attraction, or association. You can add "Products/Services" (e.g., room rates or tour packages) which appear in the public details view. Use the Performance Analytics tab to see how many travelers are viewing your listing.

AI Trip Planner

Powered by Gemini. Enter your budget, duration, and interests. The AI scans our database to suggest real Sarawak locations. You can "Save" these to your personal Itinerary which is stored securely in your account.

6. Grant Lifecycle

Grant applications follow a strictly regulated path to ensure transparency and accountability:

  1. Submission: User fills the application form. The status is "Pending". Admins are notified.
  2. Conditional Offer: Admin reviews the project and sets an "Approved Amount". The user receives a notification and must click "Accept" to proceed.
  3. Early Reporting (First 80%): After acceptance, the user must upload an "Early Report". Once Admin approves this, the first disbursement (80% of approved total) is triggered.
  4. Final Reporting (Remaining 20%): Near the project end date, the user uploads the "Final Report".
  5. Completion: Admin verifies the final report and triggers the remaining 20% disbursement. The application status becomes "Complete".

IMPORTANT LIMITATIONS

If a report is rejected 3 times, the application is automatically closed. Applicants must ensure documentation is accurate and follows the official MTCP guidelines linked on the Grants page.

7. AI Collaboration Tools

INTOURCAMS features a "Human-in-the-loop" AI design. This is most prominent in the Statistics module:

Deep Research

AI doesn't just look at our database; it performs real-time web research to identify regional trends, competitor performance, and upcoming global tourism shifts relevant to Sarawak.

Human Synthesis

Admins can manually edit AI reports. Use the "Refine with AI" button to have Gemini polish your rough bullet points into a professional executive summary.

8. Admin Panel

Website Management

Admins can set a global Site-Wide Banner for urgent news. They can also activate Maintenance Mode, which redirects all non-admin traffic to a splash screen with a custom message. This is essential for safe database updates.

User Auditing & Tiers

The User Management view allows Admins to upgrade users to "Premium" tier. Premium users gain access to the "Trends Analytics" and "ROI Statistics" views, which contain sensitive financial and engagement data.

Batch Operations

Use the "Batch Upload" feature in the Clusters view to add hundreds of locations via CSV. Download the template first to ensure column headers match exactly.

9. Security & Privacy

We use Row Level Security (RLS) to ensure data isolation. A "Tourism Player" can only see the edit buttons for the clusters they own. Grant document uploads are stored in private, encrypted buckets. When an Admin views your report, the system generates a temporary, single-use URL that expires after 60 seconds to prevent unauthorized file sharing.

10. Troubleshooting

"I am a Tourism Player but don't see the Manage menu."

Verify your role under Settings → Profile. If it says "User", an Admin must manually verify your credentials and upgrade your role in the User Management view. Verification typically takes 24-48 hours.

"The AI Planner says Quota Exceeded."

Deep research tasks utilize a shared global API quota. If exceeded, the system enters a 45-second cooldown. Simply wait a moment and try again. This does not affect your personal account status.

"My grant application disappeared."

Check the Status Filter on the Grant Applications page. By default, it may be showing "In Progress". If your application was "Rejected" or "Completed", you must set the filter to "All" or the specific status to see it in the list.

"I can't upload my report PDF."

Ensure the file size is under 5MB and is a standard PDF format. If you have already been rejected 3 times for that stage, the upload button will be permanently disabled for that specific application.

11. Support & Contact

For urgent technical issues (system crashes, login failures), please email our engineering team at intourcams@gmail.com.

For suggestions on new tourism features or UI improvements, please use the System Feedback tool located in your Profile Settings. Submissions are reviewed weekly by the system editors.
